name: Framework Laptop (13 / 16, AMD Ryzen / Intel Core)
description: Framework laptops ship Insyde H2O UEFI firmware with Framework-customised splash + board-specific modules. Both the Intel and AMD variants use Nuvoton NPCT7xx discrete TPMs.
secure-boot-default: enabled (UEFI Secure Boot with Microsoft UEFI CA + Framework Platform Key)
tpm-attestation-note: Framework publishes EK root CA chain via their community support portal; end users can extract the per-device EK cert via `tpm2_getekcertificate` or the `tpm2_nvread` path for NV 0x01C00002.
notes: Framework boards get BIOS updates via the UEFI Capsule path + LVFS (Linux Vendor Firmware Service). Each release is documented at https://knowledgebase.frame.work/en_us/framework-laptop-bios-releases-S1dMQt6F — verifiers can pin a specific BIOS revision against the known-good CRTM digest.
source: Framework community forum firmware release notes; LVFS firmware catalogue; observed CRTM + TPM vendor ID from Framework 13 AMD Ryzen owner-submitted logs.
